title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was killed in a daytime shooting on York Street in Dorchester on Wednesday morning.
Boston EMS pronounced the man dead at the scene; police have not yet identified the victim publicly.
Authorities have not made an arrest; they urge anyone with information to contact the Boston Police CrimeStoppers Program.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: U.S. Marshals arrested Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris for charges related to a June 27 shooting in Mattapan.
Wilcox-Buford faces charges including armed assault to murder; Burris is charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Both suspects were arraigned, pled not guilty, and held on bail; court hearings scheduled for August 4.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reported 18 cases of cyclosporiasis from May 1 to July 1, consistent with normal seasonal levels.
The cyclospora outbreak affecting the Midwest has not impacted Massachusetts, DPH confirms no surge in local cases.
Cyclospora infections, often linked to fresh produce, are tracked by DPH through local reports and patient interviews.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 250 event is encouraging local youth sailing programs to adapt despite large harbor crowds and logistics challenges.
Courageous Sailing Center continued its programs by towing boats to the Mystic River, avoiding the congestion of visiting Tall Ships.
Piers Park Sailing Center had 300 youths out on the water, with instructors using 23-foot Sonars for navigation in Boston Harbor.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts issued a statewide air quality alert through 12 a.m. Thursday due to Canadian wildfire smoke.
MassDEP warns elevated PM2.5 levels may affect sensitive groups, including those with heart or lung diseases.
Residents advised to limit outdoor activities, especially children, older adults, and those with respiratory conditions.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Dutch tall ship Oosterschelde is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026, offering public tours.
Oosterschelde, a three-masted schooner built in 1918, is the only remaining ship from its historical Dutch fleet.
Oosterschelde will conduct additional tours in Quincy from July 16-19 before sailing to Canada, Spain, and France.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room during a 2024 performance at the Outlaw Music Fest.
Bob Dylan invited Brittney Spencer to open for him on tour after a two-year silence since their first meeting.
Brittney Spencer touches on her inspirations, including The Chicks and Taylor Swift, and her family's storytelling legacy.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: OpenTable's 2026 list includes five New England hotel restaurants in Massachusetts, Maine, and Connecticut.
Top New England spots are Contessa and Mooo Beacon Hill in Boston, and Michael Jordan’s Steak House in Connecticut.
Maine's acclaimed entries are Earth at Hidden Pond in Kennebunkport and White Barn Inn Restaurant in Kennebunk.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, head coach of New England Revolution, shared memories of the World Cup from his childhood in Belgrade.
Mitrović reflected on watching the 1990 World Cup match between Yugoslavia and Argentina during his aunt's wedding.
He coached Serbia to victory in the 2015 FIFA U20 World Cup and the US in the 2024 Summer Olympics.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics head coach Joe Mazzulla trains in Brazilian jiu-jitsu with MMA trainer Eric Nicksick.
Mazzulla credits MMA training for improving his physical and mental edge in coaching basketball.
Mazzulla studies in-fight communication to enhance in-game communication with Celtics players.
